channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police Recover Head of Murdered Nakuru Security Guard During Investigation
item.description: Detectives in Nakuru County have recovered the missing head of a security guard who was brutally murdered earlier this week. The guard, who worked for a private security firm, disappeared last weekend...

item.title: Cuban Pediatric Heart Hospital Struggles to Provide Care Amid Fuel Shortages
item.description: Doctors and staff at the William Soler Pediatric Cardiocenter in Havana are confronting severe challenges due to the ongoing US fuel blockade. The hospital, which specialises in treating newborns, chi...

item.title: Bafana Bafana Struggle Against Panama in Cape Town Friendly
item.description: Bafana Bafana suffered a 2-1 defeat against Panama in a friendly at Cape Town Stadium on Tuesday, according to coach Hugo Broos. Broos stated that his side missed three early chances which could have ...

item.title: NASA Begins Liquid Hydrogen Chilldown for Artemis II Mission to Lunar Orbit
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission is set for launch on 2nd April 2026 at 6:24 p.m. EDT (3:54 a.m. IST). This mission marks the first time in over fifty years that humans will travel near the moon, although no...

item.title: TEEA calls for strong partnership with Ministry to protect environment in mining operations
item.description: DODOMA: Tanzania Environmental Experts Association (TEEA) imeomba kushirikiana kwa karibu na Ministry of Minerals ili kuhakikisha shughuli zote za uchimbaji na processing ya madini zinazingatia uhifad...

item.title: U.S. Nuclear Policy Shows Contradiction in Addressing Allies and Adversaries
item.description: During a congressional hearing, Representative Joaquin Castro questioned Thomas DiNanno, a senior U.S. official on arms control, about Israel's nuclear capabilities. DiNanno avoided giving a direct an...

item.title: Spring blossoms in Gilgit-Baltistan bring hope and beauty to Pakistan’s north
item.description: As the winter frost melts across the rugged peaks of Gilgit-Baltistan, springtime paints the valleys of northern Pakistan in shades of pink and white. The annual cherry and apricot blossom season has ...

item.title: Woman in her 80s dies after being stabbed in Plumstead
item.description: A woman in her 80s has tragically died following a stabbing incident in Plumstead, southeast London. Police were alerted at 8:50am to reports of a man armed with a knife at Red Lion Place. Emergency s...

item.title: Kanimozhi Highlights Public Support for DMK Manifesto in Thoothukudi
item.description: In Thoothukudi, DMK leader and MP Kanimozhi Karunanidhi expressed that the public is widely appreciating the DMK manifesto, with limited interest in the manifestos of other parties. During the inaugur...

item.title: Final Episode of 'What Lies Beneath' Brings Justice for Edong and Louisa
item.description: The popular ABS-CBN drama series “What Lies Beneath” reached its much-anticipated finale, bringing closure and justice to the characters Edong and Louisa. Throughout the series, viewers followed a com...

item.title: Farrer by-election delays implementation of new donation disclosure rules
item.description: The Australian Electoral Commission (AEC) has requested the government to delay major reforms to donation disclosure rules due to the pressures of the upcoming Farrer by-election. Initially scheduled ...

item.title: Ryanair cautions potential jet fuel supply issues in Europe due to Middle East conflict
item.description: Ryanair CEO Michael O'Leary has warned that jet fuel supplies in Europe could face disruptions in early May if the ongoing conflict in the Middle East continues. The warning comes after oil prices sur...

item.title: Government rolls out digital platform to speed up pension processing for retirees
item.description: The Kenyan government has unveiled a new digital system that aims to ease the long-standing struggles faced by pensioners when claiming their benefits. For years, retired public servants have gone thr...

item.title: Infinix Launches NOTE 60 Pro to Support Kenyan Digital Creators
item.description: Kenya’s creative industry is booming, largely thanks to a young, tech-savvy population, with over three-quarters of citizens under 35. Smartphones now serve as the main tools for content creation, mak...

item.title: Philippine Lotto Winning Numbers for April 1, 2026
item.description: ABS-CBN News has published the official lotto results for April 1, 2026, providing the latest winning numbers from the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The article informs readers about t...

item.title: Anna Magari driving people-first growth at Serengeti Breweries
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Anna Magari, Senior Human Resources Business Partner at Serengeti Breweries Limited, has been transforming how the company approaches performance by putting people first. With over ten...

item.title: Punjab Announces Four-Day School Week Amid Global Fuel Crisis
item.description: The Punjab government has announced that all public and private schools across the province will now stay closed every Friday until further notice. The decision, shared by Punjab Education Minister Ra...

item.title: Motorist and Traffic Police Clash on Jogoo Road Amid Crowds
item.description: A tense scene erupted along Jogoo Road in Nairobi when a motorist got into a heated argument with traffic police officers. According to a video circulating online, the man was visibly upset, shouting ...

item.title: BBC confirms awareness of 2017 police inquiry into Scott Mills before his 2026 dismissal
item.description: The BBC has confirmed it was aware of a police investigation involving Radio 2 presenter Scott Mills as far back as 2017, though the probe concluded in 2019 without any charges. Mills, aged 53, was di...

item.title: Police respond to teenage gatherings causing disturbances in London and Birmingham over Easter
item.description: During the Easter holiday period, London and Birmingham have seen large groups of teenagers converge in town centres, causing widespread disorder. Social media has been identified as a key factor in o...
